rule: Only mark verified after the user-visible acceptance check passes and evidence is recorded.
parallel_rule: Use a separate worktree per task; parallelize only when dependencies are verified and file ownership does not overlap.

features.title: Codex-derived Elpis foundation
features.user_visible_behavior: Elpis launches from code contained in its own repository and retains Codex-quality streaming, tools, sessions, and TUI behavior without depending on the donor clone path.
acceptance: Build and launch Elpis from a clean checkout.
Create a file and run a command through the Codex runtime.
Prove no runtime file is loaded from the donor clone directory.

features.title: Readable command and file activity
features.user_visible_behavior: The screen clearly shows command start, live output, completion or failure, file changes, and useful diffs.
acceptance: Run one command and create one file; visually inspect the complete lifecycle.
evidence: Current prototype receives events but renders them incompletely.

features.title: Exact permission modes
features.user_visible_behavior: Read Only, Default, and Full Access behave like their Codex equivalents and the active mode is always visible.
acceptance: Exercise an in-workspace edit, outside-workspace edit, command, and network access under every preset.
evidence: Prototype always starts Codex Default; its yolo toggle does not reconfigure the thread.

features.title: Reliable text selection and copying
features.user_visible_behavior: The user can select arbitrary visible text with the mouse and copy it using the terminal's normal workflow.
acceptance: Select part of an agent message with the mouse and paste exactly that selection elsewhere.
evidence: User test failed on 2026-07-15; existing docs incorrectly claim this works.

features.title: Explicit model and runtime selection
features.user_visible_behavior: The user can choose Codex, Gemini, Claude, or another supported runtime and can see which layer owns the current turn.
acceptance: Complete one visible turn on each supported runtime and resume its Elpis session.
evidence: Codex launches by default; older Python provider paths exist but parity is unverified.

features.title: Visible model context ledger
features.user_visible_behavior: The user can inspect every active context item, its source, size, admission reason, lifetime, and replacement receipt.
acceptance: Compare the visible ledger with the exact next model request and find no undisclosed item.

features.title: Expiry, receipts, and compaction
features.user_visible_behavior: Raw exploration expires, compact receipts remain, recent turns survive compaction, and the active goal is not lost.
acceptance: Run a tool-heavy task across compaction and verify the next request contains receipts instead of old raw output.
evidence: Specified only; display trimming is not model-context trimming.

features.title: Curated cross-session memory
features.user_visible_behavior: Elpis records dated notes, retrieves relevant excerpts with provenance, promotes repeatedly useful knowledge, and permits review or deletion.
acceptance: Teach a project fact, start a new session, retrieve it for a related task, and omit it for an unrelated task.
evidence: Older Python memory modules are not active in the Codex path.

features.title: Enforced creator behavior
features.user_visible_behavior: Applicable creator and project rules reach every runtime; enforceable rules are checked by code and violations are visible.
acceptance: Run the same behavioral evaluation against two runtimes and record pass/fail evidence.
evidence: Markdown guidance exists; runtime-wide enforcement and evaluation do not.

features.title: Installable and shippable product
features.user_visible_behavior: A new user can install, authenticate, launch, understand permissions, and complete a first task from a clean machine.
acceptance: Clean-environment install and first-task smoke complete without repository-specific paths.
